title: Speaking: Shopping and Ordering
learningGoal: Engage in a roleplay where you state your desires using the 'chahiye' construction and negotiate using opposites like cheap and expensive.
instructions: You are at a local market. A shopkeeper asks what you want. You will negotiate for a cheap shirt and ask for cold water.
speakingPrompt: Speak clearly. Remember to use 'Mujhe' instead of 'Main' when asking for things.
scenario: Buying items at a market stall.

subTasks.prompt: The shopkeeper says: 'Aapko kya chahiye?' (What do you want?). Reply: 'I want a shirt.' (To me a shirt is needed).
The shopkeeper shows you a shirt. Say: 'This is expensive.'
Tell the shopkeeper exactly what you want: 'I want a cheap shirt.'
The shopkeeper gives you the shirt. Now ask for water: 'I want cold water.'

acceptableParaphrases: Adding 'ek' (one/a) is fine: Mujhe ek sasti shirt chahiye.

criteria.name: Dative Pronoun Adherence
criteria.description: If the user says 'Main shirt chahiye', they fail the task immediately.

criteria.name: Adjective Gender Match
criteria.description: Must use 'sasti' with shirt (feminine) and 'thanda' with water (masculine).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Mujhe shirt chahiye. Yeh mahangi hai. Mujhe sasti shirt chahiye. Mujhe thanda paani chahiye.

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant negotiating skills! You used 'Mujhe' and 'Chahiye' perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the basic structure for 'I want'. It must ALWAYS be 'Mujhe [item] chahiye'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Watch your adjective endings. Water is masculine, so it is Thanda. Shirt is feminine, so it is Sasti.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: मुझे शर्ट चाहिए। यह महँगी है। मुझे सस्ती शर्ट चाहिए। मुझे ठंडा पानी चाहिए।

hintText: Speak slowly. Make sure the 'jh' in 'Mujhe' is aspirated (breathy).
commonMistakes: Saying 'Mujhe sasta shirt chahiye' (failing to make the adjective feminine).
